req.title: Inter-facility referral (boundary)
req.statement: A clinician shall send a referral to another facility; the referral shall trigger a secure message and consent check. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers inter-facility referral creation with three orthogonal concerns: (1) happy-path referral submission by an authenticated clinician triggering storage and secure message dispatch (EP), (2) consent-absent rejection returning 403 (NEG/DT), and (3) timestamp and numeric boundary values at the edges of accepted ranges (BVA). A fourth test covers the state transition from 'draft' to 'active' referral status (STC) to ensure the workflow gate is exercised. Together these four tests satisfy the minimum three required while spanning all five heuristic families cited in the brief.

tests.name: test_valid_referral_creates_record_and_dispatches_message
preconditions: Authenticated clinician session token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
Target facility ID exists in the system
Patient consent record marked 'granted' in the consent store
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with valid payload including patientId, targetFacilityId, priority='routine', and ISO-8601 requestedDate
Poll <secure_message_stub> for dispatched message matching referralId
expected: Secure message stub received exactly one message with matching referralId within timeout
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite:8080"

def test_valid_referral_creates_record_and_dispatches_message(
    clinician_auth_token, patient_with_consent, target_facility_id, secure_message_stub
):
    payload = {
        "patientId": patient_with_consent,
        "targetFacilityId": target_facility_id,
        "priority": "routine",
        "requestedDate": "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z",
        "priority_score": 5
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/json"}

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/ReferralRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "id" in body, "Response must contain referral id"
    assert body.get("status") == "active"

    referral_id = body["id"]
    messages = secure_message_stub.received_for(referral_id, timeout=5)
    assert len(messages) == 1, f"Expected 1 secure message, found {len(messages)}"

tests.name: test_referral_without_consent_returns_403
preconditions: Authenticated clinician session token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
Target facility ID exists in the system
Patient consent record marked 'denied' or absent in the consent store
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with valid structural payload but patientId whose consent is absent/denied
Capture response status and error body
expected: No referral record persisted in the store
trace: R-HLT-042: error pathway - 403 when consent absent
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite:8080"

def test_referral_without_consent_returns_403(
    clinician_auth_token, patient_without_consent, target_facility_id, secure_message_stub
):
    payload = {
        "patientId": patient_without_consent,
        "targetFacilityId": target_facility_id,
        "priority": "routine",
        "requestedDate": "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z",
        "priority_score": 5
    }
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/json"}

    resp = requests.post(f"{BASE_URL}/ReferralRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)

    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("errorCode") == "CONSENT_REQUIRED"

    messages = secure_message_stub.received_for(None, timeout=2)
    assert messages == [], "No message should be dispatched on consent failure"

tests.name: test_referral_with_boundary_timestamp_and_priority_score
preconditions: Authenticated clinician session token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
actions: POST /ReferralRequest with requestedDate set to exact midnight boundary '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z'
Set priority_score to minimum boundary value 1
POST /ReferralRequest again with requestedDate at max representable date '9999-12-31T23:59:59Z' and priority_score at maximum 10
expected: Requests with priority_score=1 and valid min-date return HTTP 201
Requests with priority_score=10 and valid max-date return HTTP 201
Request with priority_score=0 returns HTTP 422 with field error on priority_score
Request with priority_score=11 returns HTTP 422 with field error on priority_score
trace: R-HLT-042: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested

tests.name: test_referral_state_transition_draft_to_active
preconditions: Authenticated clinician session token available via <clinician_auth_token> fixture
A referral in 'draft' status exists (created via <create_draft_referral> fixture)
actions: PATCH /ReferralRequest/{id} with body {"status": "active"} to transition draft to active
Verify secure message is dispatched only on transition to active, not on draft creation
expected: Secure message dispatched exactly once after the PATCH transition
No secure message was dispatched during draft creation
trace: R-HLT-042: trigger - clinician sends referral (final submission from draft)
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

BASE_URL = "http://fhir-lite:8080"

def test_referral_state_transition_draft_to_active(
    clinician_auth_token, create_draft_referral, secure_message_stub
):
    referral_id = create_draft_referral
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {clinician_auth_token}",
               "Content-Type": "application/json"}

    pre_messages = secure_message_stub.received_for(referral_id, timeout=1)
    assert pre_messages == [], "No message should be sent while referral is draft"

    patch_resp = requests.patch(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ReferralRequest/{referral_id}",
        json={"status": "active"},
        headers=headers
    )
    assert patch_resp.status_code == 200, f"PATCH failed: {patch_resp.text}"

    get_resp = requests.get(
        f"{BASE_URL}/ReferralRequest/{referral_id}", headers=headers
    )
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json().get("status") == "active"

    post_messages = secure_message_stub.received_for(referral_id, timeout=5)
    assert len(post_messages) == 1, f"Expected 1 message after activation, got {len(post_messages)}"
